root_text.an4.41:1.3: Atthi, bhikkhave, samādhibhāvanā bhāvitā bahulīkatā diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārāya saṁvattati;
root_text.an4.41:1.4: atthi, bhikkhave, samādhibhāvanā bhāvitā bahulīkatā ñāṇadassanappaṭilābhāya saṁvattati;
root_text.an4.41:1.5: atthi, bhikkhave, samādhibhāvanā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satisampajaññāya saṁvattati;
root_text.an4.41:1.6: atthi, bhikkhave, samādhibhāvanā bhāvitā bahulīkatā āsavānaṁ khayāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an4.41:2.1: Katamā ca, bhikkhave, samādhibhāvanā bhāvitā bahulīkatā diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārāya saṁvattati?
root_text.an4.41:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi …pe… catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.41:2.3: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, samādhibhāvanā bhāvitā bahulīkatā diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an4.41:3.1: Katamā ca, bhikkhave, samādhibhāvanā bhāvitā bahulīkatā ñāṇadassanappaṭilābhāya saṁvattati?
root_text.an4.41:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ālokasaññaṁ manasi karoti, divāsaññaṁ adhiṭṭhāti—
root_text.an4.41:3.3: yathā divā tathā rattiṁ, yathā rattiṁ tathā divā.
root_text.an4.41:3.4: Iti vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.an4.41:3.5: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, samādhibhāvanā bhāvitā bahulīkatā ñāṇadassanappaṭilābhāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an4.41:4.1: Katamā ca, bhikkhave, samādhibhāvanā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satisampajaññāya saṁvattati?
root_text.an4.41:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno viditā vedanā uppajjanti, viditā upaṭṭhahanti, viditā abbhatthaṁ gacchanti;
root_text.an4.41:4.3: viditā saññā …pe… viditā vitakkā uppajjanti, viditā upaṭṭhahanti, viditā abbhatthaṁ gacchanti.
root_text.an4.41:4.4: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, samādhibhāvanā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satisampajaññāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an4.41:5.1: Katamā ca, bhikkhave, samādhibhāvanā bhāvitā bahulīkatā āsavānaṁ khayāya saṁvattati?
root_text.an4.41:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcasu upādānakkhandhesu udayabbayānupassī viharati:
root_text.an4.41:5.3: ‘iti rūpaṁ, iti rūpassa samudayo, iti rūpassa atthaṅgamo;
root_text.an4.41:5.4: iti vedanā, iti vedanāya samudayo, iti vedanāya atthaṅgamo;
root_text.an4.41:5.5: iti saññā, iti saññāya samudayo, iti saññāya atthaṅgamo;
root_text.an4.41:5.6: iti saṅkhārā, iti saṅkhārānaṁ samudayo, iti saṅkhārānaṁ atthaṅgamo;
root_text.an4.41:5.7: iti viññāṇaṁ, iti viññāṇassa samudayo, iti viññāṇassa atthaṅgamo’ti.
root_text.an4.41:5.8: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, samādhibhāvanā bhāvitā bahulīkatā āsavānaṁ khayāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an4.41:5.10: Idañca pana metaṁ, bhikkhave, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ pārāyane puṇṇakapañhe:

translation_text.an4.41:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four ways of developing immersion further.
translation_text.an4.41:1.3: There is a way of developing immersion further that leads to blissful meditation in this life.
translation_text.an4.41:1.4: There is a way of developing immersion further that leads to gaining knowledge and vision.
translation_text.an4.41:1.5: There is a way of developing immersion further that leads to mindfulness and awareness.
translation_text.an4.41:1.6: There is a way of developing immersion further that leads to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an4.41:2.1: And what is the way of developing immersion further that leads to blissful meditation in this life?
translation_text.an4.41:2.2: It’s when a mendicant, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters and remains in the first absorption … second absorption … third absorption … fourth absorption.
translation_text.an4.41:2.3: This is the way of developing immersion further that leads to blissful meditation in this life.
translation_text.an4.41:3.1: And what is the way of developing immersion further that leads to gaining knowledge and vision?
translation_text.an4.41:3.2: It’s when a mendicant applies their mind to the perception of light, focusing on the perception of day:
translation_text.an4.41:3.3: as by day, so by night; as by night, so by day.
translation_text.an4.41:3.4: And so, with an open and unenveloped heart, they develop a mind that’s full of radiance.
translation_text.an4.41:3.5: This is the way of developing immersion further that leads to gaining knowledge and vision.
translation_text.an4.41:4.1: And what is the way of developing immersion further that leads to mindfulness and awareness?
translation_text.an4.41:4.2: It’s when a mendicant knows feelings as they arise, as they remain, and as they go away.
translation_text.an4.41:4.3: They know perceptions as they arise, as they remain, and as they go away. They know thoughts as they arise, as they remain, and as they go away.
translation_text.an4.41:4.4: This is the way of developing immersion further that leads to mindfulness and awareness.
translation_text.an4.41:5.1: And what is the way of developing immersion further that leads to the ending of defilements?
translation_text.an4.41:5.2: It’s when a mendicant meditates observing rise and fall in the five grasping aggregates.
translation_text.an4.41:5.3: ‘Such is form, such is the origin of form, such is the ending of form.
translation_text.an4.41:5.4: Such is feeling, such is the origin of feeling, such is the ending of feeling.
translation_text.an4.41:5.5: Such is perception, such is the origin of perception, such is the ending of perception.
translation_text.an4.41:5.6: Such are choices, such is the origin of choices, such is the ending of choices.
translation_text.an4.41:5.7: Such is consciousness, such is the origin of consciousness, such is the ending of consciousness.’
translation_text.an4.41:5.8: This is the way of developing immersion further that leads to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an4.41:5.9: These are the four ways of developing immersion further.
translation_text.an4.41:5.10: And it was in this connection that I said in ‘The Way to the Far Shore’, in ‘The Questions of Puṇṇaka’:
translation_text.an4.41:6.1: ‘Having appraised the world high and low,
translation_text.an4.41:6.2: there is nothing in the world that disturbs them.
translation_text.an4.41:6.3: Peaceful, unclouded, untroubled, <j>with no need for hope,
translation_text.an4.41:6.4: they’ve crossed over rebirth and old age, I declare.’”
